comment: Declarative candidate registry for extra/qk_decode_eval.py. Each candidate declares its ladder rungs, thresholds, and the historical verdict the evaluator must reproduce. No defaults are changed by running these.

candidates.description: Current default decode route (gqa_coop_vec, FLASH_L=128, no q8). Establishes the canonical curve AND the whole-decode W==D auto-clock reproducibility band (the falsifier).
candidates.historical_note: default ~68.1/66.4/60.7 tok/s @512/1024/4096; not a promotion candidate

candidates.description: More KV-splits on the existing winner gqa_coop_vec via FLASH_L=64. Known LOCAL-PASS / W==D-FAIL: ~1.08x attention @ctx1024 (byte-exact) but +1.8%@1024 / -1.2%@4096 whole-decode (below the >=5% bar). DO NOT PROMOTE.

Warp-cooperative non-WMMA vector flash-decode tile (raw-C, standalone decode-shape A/B). Known FAIL: byte-exact but ~0.60x @ctx1024 vs gqa_coop_vec (work-bound per-key warp-reduce). Wraps the whole script as one fixed candidate.
candidates.historical_note: first_gate_pass=false; ctx1024 best_speedup_vs_coop ~0.596

candidates.description: q8 FFN handwritten route (Q8_FFN_HANDWRITTEN=1), opt-in only. Known PASS_OPT_IN: ~1.06x whole-decode W==D + dNLL 0.0029 <= 0.01. Not a default promotion (opt-in).
candidates.historical_note: speedup_W ~1.06; dNLL 0.00288; opt-in default-off

candidates.description: North-star flash_attn_tile decode candidate (warp-cooperative q.k partial + many-workgroup combine) per binding north_star_flash_attn_tile_v0. Local A/B vs gqa_coop_vec. MEASURED FAIL_LOCAL_AB: 0.58x@ctx1024, 0.89x@ctx4096 (byte-exact). The warp partial alone already exceeds coop; the combine is bandwidth-bound (more workgroups do not help) -> refutes the prior serial-combine-ceiling hypothesis.
candidates.historical_note: 0.58x@1024 / 0.89x@4096 vs gqa_coop_vec; byte-exact; local gate missed -> no W==D route

candidates.description: Path A: fused inline-exp softmax+V tail (coop matmul q.k + flash_prob fused into the partial, keep flash_max + GQA reuse) vs gqa_coop_vec. MEASURED FAIL_LOCAL_AB: 0.725x@ctx1024 / 0.876x@ctx4096 (value-correct rel_rmse 8e-4). Per-d-lane exp redundancy (W=129 lanes recompute exp) > the saved prob materialization. Full online-max removal is BLOCKED_BY_IDIOM (per-split pm + per-d pout = two-granularity store wall).
candidates.historical_note: 0.725x@1024 / 0.876x@4096 vs gqa_coop_vec; fusing exp into the partial loses (redundant per-lane exp); coop's hoisted-exp split stands

candidates.description: ISA-justified diagnostic: replace coop's scalar flash_partial (PV, 24.7us, 0 v_dot2) with PV=prob@V as a SPLIT-preserving tiled matmul. MEASURED gate FAIL_LOCAL_AB (0.879/0.936/1.131x @ctx512/1024/4096, value-correct rel_rmse 7-8e-4) BUT project verdict MATMUL_PV_BLOCKED_BY_LAYOUT: the per-split matmul (K=L=128 concrete, Hkv*Smax=256 wg) TILES at ~1078 GFLOPS and WINS 1.13x @ctx4096; it loses at ctx1024/512 only because tinygrad cannot express a symbolic-count tiled batched matmul, forcing concrete Smax=32 (full-MAXC reads = 4-8x extra split work). Corrects an earlier non-split form (~50 GFLOPS, parallelism-collapsed Hkv=8 wg) that wrongly blamed skinny M=4. No promotion; bounded lever exhausted (needs deep fused-flash codegen).
candidates.historical_note: 0.936x@1024 / 1.131x@4096 vs gqa_coop_vec; split-preserving matmul PV TILES (~1078 GFLOPS) and wins at ctx4096; BLOCKED_BY_LAYOUT (symbolic split count -> concrete Smax full-MAXC reads) loses the ctx1024 gate

candidates.description: REFERENCE ORACLE (non-default, non-promotable): llama.cpp flash_attn_tile decode attention vs gqa_coop_vec, pure GPU time (llama rocprofv3 trace vs coop ProfileGraphEvent). Answers whether llama's win is standalone-kernel or in-model. MEASURED: llama 5.7x faster @ctx1024 standalone -> PASS_ORACLE_LOCAL_AB. Vendored llama reference, NOT a tinygrad primitive; never a default route.
candidates.historical_note: llama 5.87/5.71/4.77x faster than coop standalone (pure GPU); reference oracle / codegen target; non-promotable

candidates.description: Evaluator-binding plumbing selftest (binding_templates.json north_star_binding_selftest_v0). Validates the binding -> candidate -> decode_eval -> artifact path WITHOUT benchmarking. Verdict SELFTEST_PASS; NOT a performance candidate.
candidates.historical_note: plumbing only; no GPU; no performance claim

candidates.description: DECISIVE post-Matmul-PV concrete-shape fused-flash first gate (literature-grounded: FlashAttention IO-aware tiling + online softmax, Flash-Decoding KV-splits, FlashDecoding++ flat-GEMM under-utilization, FlashInfer decode-phase). CONCRETE ctx1024 flash-decode pipeline whose q.k AND PV ride tinygrad's tiled-GEMM codegen (S=Tc/L=8 FAIR splits -- the matmul-PV symbolic-split BLOCKER removed by fixing the shape). MEASURED FAIL_LOCAL_AB: 0.965x @ctx1024 vs strict same-shape concrete gqa_coop_vec (value-correct rel_rmse 4.9e-4). The 1.42x vs the canonical SYMBOLIC comparator is a concreteness artifact (the ~27us symbolic generalization tax the candidate would also pay once generalized), NOT a win. ROOT CAUSE: at decode shape tinygrad's tiled matmul renders REGISTER-tiled global-load code (16 wg, 305 GFLOPS, no __shared__/LDS, no v_dot2) -- NOT llama's one-kernel LDS-staged v_dot2 fused tile; the under-utilization + 2 extra layout kernels offset the tiling benefit. The TRUE single fused LDS-tiled kernel stays inexpressible (tiled-GEMM codegen is per-matmul/separate-kernel; .set/.after fusion is scalar -- mutually exclusive). Gate fails -> REST_DECODE + v2.
candidates.historical_note: 0.965x @ctx1024 vs concrete gqa_coop_vec (same fixed shape); register-tiled not LDS-tiled; FlashDecoding++ flat-GEMM under-utilization at 16 wg; FUSED_FLASH_CONCRETE_GATE_FAIL_LOCAL_AB

candidates.description: REFERENCE ORACLE (non-default, non-promotable): vendored llama.cpp flash_attn_tile<128,128,1,4,false> + combine launched THROUGH tinygrad's HCQ (capture-and-replay) vs gqa_coop_vec, GPU-busy time. Route B B1 de-risk: does the llama-class kernel win when DISPATCHED BY tinygrad's runtime? MEASURED 2.96x GPU-time @ctx1024 -> PASS_ORACLE_LOCAL_AB; BUT wall is launch-overhead-bound (2 raw HCQ dispatches) so W==D needs graph integration. Vendored llama, never a default route.
candidates.historical_note: Route B B1: vendored llama tile via HCQ wins 2.96x GPU-time @ctx1024 (correct rel 1.3e-3); wall launch-overhead-bound -> W==D needs graph integration; non-promotable | B2 (launch integration): folding tile+combine into ONE bound HCQ queue (1 doorbell, baked kernargs) -> integrated WALL 1.65x FASTER than coop (~36 vs 60us; B1 was 0.39x) -> B2_LOCAL_GRAPH_PASS; GPU win survives integration; Route B viable -> B3 owned AMDGCN. Vendored W==D layout-blocked.

candidates.description: OWNED, PROMOTABLE hand-AMDGCN decode-attention tile (extra/qk_owned_flash_decode.hip) for tinygrad's NATIVE K/V layout [Hkv,MAXC,Hd]. llama-style dataflow (warp-per-q-head + GQA-pack LDS staging, v_dot2 q.k, register online softmax+PV, KV-split+combine) authored from scratch (NOT vendored llama code). B3 LOCAL A/B: beats gqa_coop_vec 2.35x GPU-busy / 1.70x wall (matched-sync) @ctx1024, correct rel 2e-7. default_eligible=false: W==D blocked by external-kernel-as-JIT-graph-node integration (not Route-A codegen).
candidates.historical_note: B3 owned AMDGCN tile: LOCAL PASS (2.35x GPU / 1.70x wall vs coop, correct rel 2e-7, v_dot2=2, 56 VGPR, 8KB LDS, 0 spill); promotable + tinygrad-native layout. W==D BLOCKED (raw HCQ kernel can't enter JIT decode graph w/o Route-A codegen or eager); default_eligible=false. decode_eval shows REST without a W==D runner. See docs/decode-attention-route-b-b3-owned-amdgcn-result-20260621.md.

candidates.description: Route B B4 follow-on: OWNED AMDGCN decode-attention route as external precompiled Ops.PROGRAM JIT graph nodes (`extra/qk_owned_flash_decode_graph_node.py`), tinygrad-native [Hkv,MAXC,Hd] layout, route-gated default-off with explicit `DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_TILE`, `DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_S`, and flash threshold flags. Local result still inherits B3; W==D is evaluated with the route active and currently misses promotion criteria.
candidates.historical_note: B4 follow-on runtime eval: route-node visibility is confirmed in-model, but fixed-policy W==D does not clear the promotion gate (best measured: +0.24%@512, -0.76%@1024, +5.36%@4096 with route-firing policy; +5.56%@4096-only without 1024 route). decode_eval currently has no B4 verdict state, so this entry is tracked as REST with compare_artifact `bench/qk-decode-attention-route-b-b4/latest.json`. default_eligible=false.
candidates.default_flip: FLIPPED 2026-06-23 (authorized): DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_TILE defaults to 1 and _init_state allocates an fp16 cache, BOTH gated to gfx1100/Qwen3-8B/B=1/T=1/Hq=32/Hkv=8/Hd=128 (every other shape/device stays gqa+fp32). DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_TILE=0 fully disables (fp32 gqa). Final confirmation on shipped code via the canonical W==D harness (extra/qk_decode_runtime_overhead.py, tok_s_W real decode): gqa 76.1/74.0/71.0/67.1 -> owned 85.8/85.4/84.3/82.1 = +12.7/+15.4/+18.7/+22.4% @ctx512/1024/2048/4096. Byte-identical to gqa: short-ctx SDPA decode [279,1156,...], ctx1024 64-tok, ctx512/2048; route fires by default, falls back at ctx<512 and on TILE=0. See docs/owned-attention-default-flip-result-20260623.md.
candidates.fo2_fp16_cache: SHIPPED (2026-06-23): DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_TILE now implies a native fp16 cache (FO2), dropping the fp32->fp16 cast copy. Byte-identical to gqa at ctx512/1024/2048; default (flag off) stays fp32 + byte-identical. W==D vs gqa (interleaved repeats, clock-controlled): +13.1%@512, +16.0%@1024, +18.8%@2048, +23.2%@4096 -- ~+5-8% over the cast route. See bench/qk-owned-amdgcn-tile-post-promotion/fp16_cache_wd.json, docs/owned-tile-post-promotion-four-step-result-20260623.md.
candidates.real_cache_status: PROMOTABLE_ALLCTX (2026-06-23): two bugs fixed -> now real-cache-correct AND W==D-positive at ALL ctx. (1) dtype-contract: owned tile reads __half but canonical cache_kv is fp32 -> the route now unconditionally casts Q/K/V to fp16 (model.py; fp16->fp16 no-op). (2) ctx1024 was blocked ONLY by an overly-conservative MIN_CTX=2048 route guard (the standalone tile is empty-split-safe; the earlier short-ctx NaN was the dtype bug) -> MIN_CTX default lowered 2048->512. REVALIDATED real chunked-prefill multi-step decode token-correctness: byte-identical to gqa (ctx512 16tok, ctx1024 64tok, ctx2048 64tok/2prompts); default gqa decode unchanged. W==D (repeated, tight): +6.1%@512, +8.4%@1024, +11.5%@2048, +15.5%@4096 -> clears +5%@1024 & +7%@4096, no ctx512 regression. default_eligible=true (gates pass, byte-identical, shape/arch-guarded, fallback-safe); default_on=false (no flip; recommend an in-process A/B before flipping; route is gfx1100/Qwen3-8B/B=1/T=1-guarded external HCQ PROGRAM node behind DECODE_ATTN_AMDGCN_TILE). The fp16 cast adds a copy on top of the materialization (a native fp16 cache, deferred FO2, would drop it). See docs/owned-amdgcn-tile-short-ctx-result-20260623.md.
candidates.split_kv_economics_audit: bench/qk-split-kv-economics-audit/latest.json -> COMBINE_TAX_DOMINATES. The W==D miss is attributed (extra/qk_split_kv_economics_audit.py): the split-KV combine is a flat latency-bound floor (~64 GB/s << 960 peak, 32 wg << 96 CUs; combine 44/35/26/17% of attention @ctx512/1024/2048/4096) -- a cheaper/fused combine is the actionable lever (Amdahl projection: ctx4096 measured +5.41% -> +6.97% half-combine -> +8.58% free-combine), Amdahl co-limits. Per split_kv_economics_contract_v1, this and any future split-KV candidate must report tile/combine economics before W==D promotion. See docs/split-kv-economics-audit-result-20260621.md.
candidates.whole_cache_buffer_identity: PROMOTED DEFAULT-ON 2026-06-23: the owned tile now reads the WHOLE cache_kv buffer (owned_flash_tile_gqa_whole, K at 0 / V at +Hkv*MAXC*Hd) instead of slice views -> removes E_49152 full-MAXC materialization. W==D +13-19% (ctx512..4096), byte-identical, tinygrad decode 102-105% of llama.cpp. Flag DECODE_ATTN_KV_IDENTITY (default 1; =0 reverts to slice route). The runtime-KV core-persistence lane is RETIRED as mis-scoped (the +11% tax was the slice read, not persistence). See docs/owned-tile-buffer-identity-kv-read-result-20260623.md + docs/decode-campaign-final-synthesis-20260623.md.

candidates.description: LOSSLESS FP Q4_K FFN weight-GEMV work-decomposition variant (q4k_gemv_warp): 32 threads/row (one gfx1100 wave) + K-block-parallel (block_group x lane4 coalesced) + IN-KERNEL warp_reduce_sum (ds_bpermute) + one output, vs the default 1-thread/row serial uncoalesced q4k_gemv_partial. llama's MMVQ work decomposition in FP (no q8, no int-dot/MMVQ). Env-gated default-off: Q4K_GEMV_WARP=1 (FFN gate/up) + Q4K_GEMV_WARP_DOWN=1 (Q4_K down). FIRST decode primitive to clear the W==D gate since the attention arc.
candidates.supported_shapes: gfx1100; FFN gate/up Q4_K 4096->12288 (parts==1), FFN down Q4_K 12288->4096 (k_blocks%4==0); kernel shape-general, route 8B-shape-guarded (14B/32B fall back to default)

research_only_expansions.Q6K_GEMV_WARP_DOWN: Q6_K down 1.09x local, already coop-served, no W==D gain
research_only_expansions.Q4K_GEMV_WARP_PROJ: attn q/o 1.32x local but attention-overlapped, no transfer

candidates.promotion_hardening: Q4K_GEMV_WARP_READY_FOR_OWNER_DEFAULT_DECISION (docs/q4k-gemv-warp-promotion-hardening-result-20260622.md): reproduced ~+9.6%@1024 / +8.5%@4096 (spread ~0.4%), real-generation byte-identical 0/64, fallback-safe. OWNER decision pending to flip default-on.
candidates.historical_note: Q4K_GEMV_WARP_WD_PASS: lossless FP work-decomposition GEMV (warp_reduce_sum) clears W==D BYTE-IDENTICALLY. gate/up+down W==D median +9.75/+9.63/+8.55% @ctx512/1024/4096 (decode 66.6->73.5 @1024, ~67%->~73% of llama); local A/B 1.31x gate/up / 1.37x down vs opted default; greedy 0 mismatches (real generation). default_eligible=true (lossless + passes + no regress) but kept DEFAULT-OFF pending owner approval. Realizes the GEMV_SCHEDULE_BOUND bounded lever (docs/decode-ffn-gemv-warp-result-20260622.md, docs/q4k-gemv-warp-promotion-hardening-result-20260622.md).

candidates.description: Default-off generated/scheduler Q4_K FFN gate/up skeleton for GEMV purity attribution. This is not a promotion candidate yet; it exists so the purity gate can distinguish generated code from owned warp GEMV and the lane-partition custom bridge.
candidates.historical_note: G1 generated-route skeleton: route attribution only. Existing scheduler-generated Q4_K packed dequant path is token-correct but far slower than owned/bridge; purity work continues in the codegen representation lane.

candidates.description: Default-off G2.3 generated/scheduler Q4_K FFN gate/up route using the bridge-independent Q4KGateUpLaneMap and packed-address builder metadata. This tests runtime/codegen binding without owned warp GEMV or lane-partition custom bridge.
candidates.historical_expected_verdict: SEARCH_GENERATED_WD_FAIL until generated load/dequant/reduce/store binding approaches the owned/FutureSight route
candidates.historical_note: G2.3 runtime/codegen binding arm: route-clean generated Tensor/scheduler path consuming the minimal Q4_K LaneMap representation. Promotion requires W==D speed near owned/FutureSight plus route attribution proving no custom bridge.

candidates.description: Default-off G3.1 generated named wave32 Q4_K FFN gate/up UOp program from Q4KGateUpLaneMap. Emits q4k_g3_lanemap_gemv_* without owned warp or lane-partition bridge route attribution.
candidates.historical_note: G3.1 W==D: 103.7/101.7/99.4/94.5-ish class, route-clean, token-correct, named generated gate/up program. Not default until BubbleBeam binding replaces the bridge.

candidates.description: Promotion-focused proof lane for the measured aggressive decode probe. This is NOT the full aggressive-target envelope proof; it asks the narrower question: is the observed ~1.6-1.8% W==D gain over the current decode baseline stable, route-clean, byte-correct, and worth promoting as the next baseline? The latest probe measured 103.4/101.6/99.1/94.4 tok/s @ctx512/1024/2048/4096 versus current baseline 101.6/99.8/97.3/92.7, but the aggressive-target proof failed because it missed the higher 104.0/102.1/99.6/95.1 envelope.

promotion_contract.authority: clean W==D PROFILE-off auto-clock tok_s_W plus decode lifecycle route/materialization/unknown-lockstep gates
promotion_contract.minimum_gain: positive at every ctx and >=1.0% median gain versus the current canonical baseline
promotion_contract.route_gate: owned_flash_tile_gqa_whole present, slice route absent, DECODE_ATTN_KV_IDENTITY=1
promotion_contract.target_separation: failure to reach the aggressive envelope does not block this lane if the smaller promotion contract passes

candidates.historical_note: Created after DECODE_AGGRESSIVE_TARGET_UNPROVEN__THROUGHPUT: aggressive probe was faster than baseline and passed route/unknown checks, but missed the higher target envelope by ~0.5-0.7%. This candidate exists to evaluate the smaller measured gain as a promotion candidate with its own acceptance contract.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention online-state PV x-lane route. Correctness gates now pass (P8/P10/P11/P12/P13/P14 + full in-model structural route clean). This candidate measures whether the generated x-lane route transfers to whole-decode W==D before any promotion decision.
candidates.historical_note: Added after x-lane route correctness fix; W==D perf is the authority for whether this becomes a promotion lane.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention split-state x-lane route. Computes split max once with the proven scalar max kernel, emits augmented x-lane PV with denominator column, then reuses existing gmax/den/combine. This tests whether removing per-output-column online-state recurrence fixes the prior x-lane W==D collapse.
candidates.historical_note: P15 numeric gate passes; W==D decides if this replaces the refuted per-d online-state x-lane route.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention fused PV tile route. Standalone numeric gate passes and route/materialization gate is clean: target program flash_fused_pv_tile_whole_cache_32_128 fires, owned tile/combine absent, refuted split x-lane PV absent, E_49152 absent, tokens match. W==D decides whether the local-d fused PV representation transfers to whole-decode speed.
candidates.historical_note: Added after FUSED_PV_TILE_ROUTE_CLEAN__WD_REQUIRED; W==D is the first speed authority.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention route that fuses q.k score reduce, online split state (l/m), and PV accumulation into one generated tile, then runs generated state gmax/combine. Standalone numeric and route/materialization gates are clean; W==D decides whether this pure-generated route is promotable.
candidates.historical_note: Route gate clean: flash_fused_score_state_pv_tile_whole_cache_32_128 + flash_state_gmax_32_128 + flash_state_combine_32_128, old score/max/den/combine absent. Standalone numeric max_abs 5.34e-05, rel_rmse 1.95e-07. Do not promote without W==D.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention route using the P1 LaneMap/CrossLane q.k score kernel (DECODE_ATTN_PHYSICAL_TILE_P1_SCORE=1). This is partial physical-primitive route integration: cross-lane score reduction is in-route; v_dot2 and LDS remain probe-visible but not in this route.
candidates.historical_note: P1 route gate clean; score numeric max_abs 4.47e-08; ISA has cross-lane ds_bpermute. Partial primitive integration only; do not promote without W==D and full primitive accounting.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention route using the PALL physical score kernel (DECODE_ATTN_PHYSICAL_TILE_PALL_SCORE=1). The score stage composes LDS-staged K, LaneMap/CrossLane reduction, and fdot2; full online state + PV lifecycle integration remains required before promotion.
candidates.historical_note: Score route gate clean when artifact verdict is PALL_SCORE_ROUTE_CLEAN__FULL_LIFECYCLE_NEXT. This is not W==D promotion-ready until online state and PV share the same physical tile lifecycle.

candidates.description: Default-off generated whole-cache decode-attention route using the PALL physical lifecycle kernel (DECODE_ATTN_PHYSICAL_TILE_PALL_LIFECYCLE=1). It composes physical q.k score, online state, and PV in one generated lifecycle with LDS, cross-lane reduction, and fdot2. Known limitation: q.k is recomputed per output column.
candidates.historical_note: Full generated lifecycle route is clean only after standalone lifecycle gate passes; speed is expected risky because q.k is still recomputed per output column.

candidates.description: REFUTED diagnostic-only chunked score-broadcast PALL lifecycle route. It computes split state once for final combine, then runs four 32-column PV chunks that recompute q.k per chunk and is W==D non-promotable.
candidates.historical_note: Refuted by decode score-broadcast lifecycle audit: correctness/capture-clean diagnostic route, but whole-cache six-kernel lifecycle is W==D non-promotable and must not be promoted. Stale FAIL_CORRECTNESS evaluator artifacts before the chunks=4 route-gate fix are superseded by this refutation.
candidates.refutation: Unfused whole-cache score-broadcast decode route is correctness/capture-clean but W==D-refuted: GPU-bound, 6 attention kernels/token, recomputes q.k per PV chunk, plus full-cache materialization risk; do not promote or tune this route.
candidates.next_dependency: CrossLaneReduceStore microgate before any FusedScorePVLifecycle attention route work
candidates.forward_plan: Do not write another score-broadcast/whole-cache attention route. First prove the cross-lane reduction/store primitive in isolation; only then rebuild the fused tile and run split-KV economics before W==D.
